expectations.text: Flags 85% concentration as exceeding the 30% single-position threshold
expectations.evidence: Flags 85% as too concentrated. Mentions 'most financial advisors would recommend (typically 5-10% max in any single stock).' Does not reference the specific 30% threshold but clearly identifies the problem.

expectations.text: Explains ASK tax rules: tax-free switching inside ASK, only taxed on withdrawal above total deposits
expectations.evidence: Dedicated section 'How ASK Works for You': 'Sell shares and reinvest within the account without triggering tax.' And: 'The tax is only triggered when you withdraw funds from the ASK, and only on gains.'

expectations.text: Notes the additional risk of having both employment income AND portfolio concentrated in the same company
expectations.evidence: 'When you hold 85% of your investments in KOG/KM and also earn your salary from KM, you have double exposure... This is sometimes called human capital correlation.'

expectations.text: Presents bull case (KOG/KM defense/maritime growth) and bear case (sector downturn, layoffs + portfolio loss simultaneously)
expectations.evidence: Separate 'Arguments for Your Current Position' (insider knowledge, growing sectors, strong performance) and 'Arguments for Diversifying' (uncompensated risk, capital protection).

expectations.text: Considers gradual diversification vs immediate rebalancing
expectations.evidence: 'Sell 15-20% of your KOG/KM position every 2-3 months until you reach your target allocation of 20-30%.' Also mentions directing new savings to diversified funds as natural dilution.

expectations.text: Models FI timeline impact of different portfolio allocations (concentrated vs diversified)
expectations.evidence: 3-scenario table (85% concentrated, 25% balanced, 100% index) with expected returns and volatility. Key line: 'the risk reduction is enormous. For financial freedom planning, reducing the chance of a catastrophic loss matters more.'

expectations.text: Conservative bias visible — recommends reducing concentration even if bull case is strong
expectations.evidence: Final answer table: 'Is 85% in KOG/KM too concentrated? Yes, especially when you also work at KM.' Recommends 20-30% target despite acknowledging 'growing sectors.'
